action.description: Implement query-time relevance feedback using pseudo-relevance feedback (PRF): take the top-3 retrieved documents from an initial retrieval pass, extract their most distinctive terms/embedding centroid, and use these to augment the original query vector before a second retrieval pass. Specifically, compute a weighted average of the original query embedding and the mean embedding of the top-3 doc chunks (e.g., 0.7 * query_vec + 0.3 * mean_top3_vec), then re-rank using this refined query vector. This is sometimes called 'RM3-style' or 'embedding-based PRF'.
